{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōtachi Kofun | native_name =甲立古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotachi Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōtachi Kofun (left is anterior, right is posterior) | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōtachi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Akitakata, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per [[electrical resistivity tomography]] investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no [[grave goods]] have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[Kōtachi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Kotachi Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120240.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.akitakata.jp/ja/shisei/section/kyouiku/shisekibunkazai/cultural_asset/siseki_kuni/e147/ Akitakata City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[Kōtachi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kōtachi Kofun | native_name =甲立古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotachi Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōtachi Kofun (left is anterior, right is posterior) | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōtachi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Akitakata, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per [[electrical resistivity tomography]] investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no [[grave goods]] have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[Kōtachi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōtachi Kofun | native_name =甲立古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotachi Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōtachi Kofun (left is anterior, right is posterior) | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōtachi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Akitakata, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per [[electrical resistivity tomography]] investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no [[grave goods]] have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[Kōtachi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Kotachi Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120240.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.akitakata.jp/ja/shisei/section/kyouiku/shisekibunkazai/cultural_asset/siseki_kuni/e147/ Akitakata City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per [[electrical resistivity tomography]] investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no [[grave goods]] have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[Kōtachi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotachi Kofun]][[en:Kōtachi Kofun]][[ja:甲立古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577967]] Wikidata name: Kotachi Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōtachi Kofun | native_name =甲立古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotachi Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōtachi Kofun (left is anterior, right is posterior) | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōtachi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.705|N|132.754694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per [[:en:electrical resistivity tomography|electrical resistivity tomography]] investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kōtachi Station|Kōtachi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotachi Kofun]][[en:Kōtachi Kofun]][[ja:甲立古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577967]] Wikidata name: Kotachi Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōtachi Kofun | native_name =甲立古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotachi Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōtachi Kofun (left is anterior, right is posterior) | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōtachi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.705|N|132.754694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per [[:en:electrical resistivity tomography|electrical resistivity tomography]] investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kōtachi Station|Kōtachi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Kotachi Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120240.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.akitakata.jp/ja/shisei/section/kyouiku/shisekibunkazai/cultural_asset/siseki_kuni/e147/ Akitakata City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotachi Kofun]][[en:Kōtachi Kofun]][[ja:甲立古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577967]] Wikidata name: Kotachi Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōtachi Kofun | native_name =甲立古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotachi Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōtachi Kofun (left is anterior, right is posterior) | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōtachi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.705|N|132.754694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per [[:en:electrical resistivity tomography|electrical resistivity tomography]] investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kōtachi Station|Kōtachi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Kotachi Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120240.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.akitakata.jp/ja/shisei/section/kyouiku/shisekibunkazai/cultural_asset/siseki_kuni/e147/ Akitakata City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per [[:en:electrical resistivity tomography|electrical resistivity tomography]] investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kōtachi Station|Kōtachi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers

The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kōtachi Station|Kōtachi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .

The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per [[:en:electrical resistivity tomography|electrical resistivity tomography]] investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kōtachi Station|Kōtachi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]]. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .

The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per {{ill|electrical resistivity tomography|en|electrical resistivity tomography|WD=Q488986|lt=electrical resistivity tomography}} investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kōtachi Station|en|Kōtachi Station|WD=Q6455309|lt=Kōtachi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Geibi Line|en|Geibi Line|WD=Q841404|lt=Geibi Line}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .

The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per {{ill|electrical resistivity tomography|en|electrical resistivity tomography|WD=Q488986|lt=electrical resistivity tomography}} investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kōtachi Station|en|Kōtachi Station|WD=Q6455309|lt=Kōtachi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Geibi Line|en|Geibi Line|WD=Q841404|lt=Geibi Line}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .

The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per {{ill|electrical resistivity tomography|en|electrical resistivity tomography|WD=Q488986|lt=electrical resistivity tomography}} investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kōtachi Station|en|Kōtachi Station|WD=Q6455309|lt=Kōtachi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Geibi Line|en|Geibi Line|WD=Q841404|lt=Geibi Line}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers

This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kōtachi Station|en|Kōtachi Station|WD=Q6455309|lt=Kōtachi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Geibi Line|en|Geibi Line|WD=Q841404|lt=Geibi Line}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .

The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per {{ill|electrical resistivity tomography|en|electrical resistivity tomography|WD=Q488986|lt=electrical resistivity tomography|1=Electrical resistivity tomography}} investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kōtachi Station|en|Kōtachi Station|WD=Q6455309|lt=Kōtachi Station|1=Kōtachi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Geibi Line|en|Geibi Line|WD=Q841404|lt=Geibi Line|1=Geibi Line}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kōtachi Kofun | native_name =甲立古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotachi Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōtachi Kofun (left is anterior, right is posterior) | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōtachi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.705|N|132.754694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|18.07|N|132|45|16.90|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per {{ill|electrical resistivity tomography|en|electrical resistivity tomography|WD=Q488986|lt=electrical resistivity tomography|1=Electrical resistivity tomography}} investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kōtachi Station|en|Kōtachi Station|WD=Q6455309|lt=Kōtachi Station|1=Kōtachi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Geibi Line|en|Geibi Line|WD=Q841404|lt=Geibi Line|1=Geibi Line}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|WD=Q9644839|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)}}

The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per {{ill|electrical resistivity tomography|en|electrical resistivity tomography|qid=Q488986|lt=electrical resistivity tomography|1=Electrical resistivity tomography}} investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
The tumulus is located approximately 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kōtachi Station|en|Kōtachi Station|qid=Q6455309|lt=Kōtachi Station|1=Kōtachi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Geibi Line|en|Geibi Line|qid=Q841404|lt=Geibi Line|1=Geibi Line}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the {{nihongo|Akitakata Museum of History and Folklore |安芸高田市歴史民俗博物館||}} .
;Total length: 77.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30.5 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 56.2 meter diameter x 8 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|qid=Q9644839|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kōtachi Kofun'''|甲立古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Kōda, Hiroshima|Kōda]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Akitakata, Hiroshima|Akitakata, Hiroshima]] in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus, which is the second largest in Hiroshima Prefecture, was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/288463|title=甲立古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century (the end of the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Kōtachi Kofun is located on a ridge to the northeast of the urban center of the former town of Kōda. It was recognized as a ''kofun'' in 2008, and [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted from 2010 to 2013, and 2018–2019. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is orientated to the south. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers in the posterior circular portion and two tiers in the anterior rectangular portion, and has a total length of 77.5 meters. The entire outer surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of both cylindrical ("morning glory-shaped" and elliptical tubular) and figurative (boat-shaped, house-shaped and armor-shaped) ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. There is no trace of a moat. Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remain uncertain, but per {{ill|electrical resistivity tomography|en|electrical resistivity tomography|qid=Q488986|lt=electrical resistivity tomography|1=Electrical resistivity tomography}} investigation, there is a pit-type stone-lined chamber in the center of the posterior circular portion. As this has yet to be excavated by digging, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been discovered.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed around the latter half of the 4th century based on its design and ''haniwa'', which are both very characteristic of the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] of Japan and indicate a strong connection to the Yamato kingdom. During later investigation, a second tumulus was discovered in the near vicinity. This was a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus which dates from the end of the 3rd century to the beginning of the 4th century.
